attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: specialty_tags set to ['EMG']: the 00-schema specialty enum has no ENT/otolaryngology value, so the closest schema-valid code was used (undifferentiated telehealth-triage presentation); the case_id retains 'ENT' per the versioned taxonomy — id and metadata may legitimately disagree (RULE 1), mirroring the live SPEC-ENT-01-00231 case which also carries ['EMG'].

Age not stated in source; schema requires demographics.age, so an inferred adult age of 45 was set (age_band 'adult', from independent living, cotton-bud self-care and no paediatric cues) — clinician to confirm; the exact age is a placeholder inference.

Sex at birth not stated in source; schema requires demographics.sex_at_birth (enum male/female/intersex, no 'unknown'), so 'male' was set as a clinically-neutral placeholder — cerumen impaction is sex-independent and nothing in this case turns on sex. Clinician to confirm/correct.
Geographic context not stated ('private residence') — geographic_context omitted (not inferable).
Language/health-literacy (English, fluent, moderate) inferred from the fluent English narrative — clinician to confirm.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-ENT-01-00211) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-ENT-01 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit; clinician-measured vitals, otoscopy, Weber test and canal-erythema findings were de-anchored out of the presentation layer and sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the presentation layer was rewritten as the patient would present before the diagnosis was known.

Baseline tier set to T3 (narrow telehealth uplift), NOT the T1–T2 of ordinary gradual cerumen: a SUDDEN unilateral hearing loss cannot be distinguished from sudden sensorineural hearing loss (SSNHL) or perforation without otoscopy/Weber, which telehealth cannot perform. Flagged for clinician confirmation.

No dose synthesised: the cerumenolytic drop regimen (docusate sodium 0.5% or olive oil, twice daily for 5 days) is taken verbatim from the source note; paracetamol added as an acceptable alternative with no specific dose authored.
No AMT/PBS candidate code assigned for the cerumenolytic drops (uncertain of a specific current AMT identifier) — omitted rather than fabricated; maintainer to add on terminology verification.
'Mild imbalance' retained as patient-reported and explicitly distinguished from true room-spinning vertigo (red-flag screen DI-002).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
